description:
video credit: @rampicshow 
removal request please ping me to myrithvik@gmail.com 

Witness a powerful moment of faith and generosity at the Sri Bhrammasakthipuram Karuppasamy temple. In a world where many focus on collections, this video captures a rare tradition where coins are distributed among devotees as a form of blessing. This selfless act has touched the hearts of many on social media, reminding us of the true spirit of devotion.

title: The Pedal-Powered Helicopter 🚁
description:
The Sikorsky prize was offered by the American Helicopter Society in 1980 as one of aviation's great unclaimed challenges. The rules sounded simple: build a helicopter powered only by a human pedaling, get it to hover for 60 seconds, reach 3 meters of altitude, and stay inside a 10-meter square. For three decades every attempt came up short. The physics work against you, a human can barely put out enough sustained power to lift their own weight off the ground using rotors, and the machine has to be absurdly light while still staying controllable in ground effect.

Atlas was built by AeroVelo, a team of engineering students from the University of Toronto. Four giant rotors, a bike frame at the center, and one pedaling pilot. On June 13, 2013, it held a 64-second hover at 3.3 meters inside the box, becoming the first human-powered helicopter in history to meet every official requirement and claiming the $250,000 prize.

Original video credit: Geeky Facts

No copyright infringement intended. Content is shared for educational purposes only. Full credit goes to the original creator. For any questions or copyright concerns, please contact me via the email in the channel description.
